questions.question: Why is plain next-token prediction a "surprisingly weak signal"?
options: Cross-entropy can't be optimized well

Each hidden state is supervised to predict exactly ONE thing, while structure, coherence, factuality, and arithmetic flow extend well beyond one token — the model must learn these by accumulating one-token signals over trillions of tokens
Tokens are too small a unit of text
Next-token loss ignores rare words
questions.explanation: MTP's question: what if every hidden state predicted multiple future tokens at once? Denser supervision per position, same data.

questions.question: Why couldn't Gloeckle et al.'s parallel MTP heads be used for speculative decoding?
options: They were too slow to evaluate

Independent heads on the same hidden state predicted different offsets WITHOUT chaining causally — head k never saw the prediction for k−1, so the multi-token outputs weren't a coherent draft sequence
Meta's license forbade it
They only predicted two tokens ahead
questions.explanation: DeepSeek-V3's redesign keeps the causal chain: depth k's hidden state is built FROM depth k−1's state combined with the embedding of the intervening token.

questions.question: How does a sequential MTP module construct its per-depth hidden state?
options: By copying the backbone state unchanged

h^(k) = T_k(M_k · concat(RMSNorm(h^(k−1)), RMSNorm(E(t_{i+k})))) — each depth gets its own small transformer block while the embedding E and output head are SHARED with the main model to keep overhead modest
By averaging all previous states
Through a frozen adapter layer
questions.explanation: At DeepSeek-V3 scale: 14B extra parameters across MTP modules on a 671B model — about 2% overhead for denser training signal plus a free inference asset.

questions.question: What double payoff did DeepSeek-V3 get from MTP?
options: Smaller checkpoints and faster loading

Training: gradient flow through the depth-k losses distills multi-step structure back into the main model; inference: the trained MTP heads are repurposed as speculative-decoding drafters with 80%+ acceptance, yielding 1.8x generation throughput
Better tokenization and longer context
Cheaper data and fewer GPUs
questions.explanation: The same module serves both phases — the rare optimization that improves the model AND ships a deployment speedup as a by-product.

questions.question: How is the MTP training loss assembled?
options: Only the deepest module is trained
A cross-entropy loss per prediction depth k = 1..D against ground-truth t_{i+k}, averaged/weighted and added to the main next-token loss — every position gets D+1 supervision signals instead of one
An adversarial loss against a critic
MSE between hidden states
questions.explanation: The joint loss across depths is the entire mechanism: more gradient information per token of data, with teacher-forced ground-truth embeddings keeping each depth's input clean.
